steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize the significance of a similarity between two kits. The notes show Pbact alone achieved both targets: high yield and purity close to the ideal of 1.8. CTAB 2X and Pplant both fell short of Pbact — that shared shortfall is the meaningful similarity to highlight.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — both CTAB 2X and Pplant exceeded yield and purity thresholds: describes a trivial overlap, not what makes the similarity significant; also may not accurately reflect the data. ✗
  • Choice B — both CTAB 2X and Pplant were less effective than Pbact due to insufficient yield or purity: captures the meaningful shared status — both kits failed to match Pbact on at least one key metric. ✓
  • Choice C — CTAB 2X and Pbact had equal purity scores: misstates the data (CTAB 2X purity was 2.2, Pbact purity was 1.6 — not equal). ✗
  • Choice D — CTAB 2X had dramatically higher yield than Pbact and Pplant: highlights only CTAB 2X's yield advantage (2,134 vs. lower figures), not a shared trait between two kits. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
